q:
travel
 Rachel began her trip three months ago. She ___ for three months.
a: She has been travelling for three months.

q:
visit
 Rachel is travelling round Europe. She ___ six countries so far.
a: She has visited six countries so far.

q:
win
 Patrick began playing tennis at age 10. He ___ the national championship four times.
a: He has won the national championship four times.

q:
play
 Patrick is a tennis player. He ___ since he was ten.
a: He has been playing since he was ten.

q:
make
 Lisa and Sue started making films after college. They ___ films since they left college.
a: They have been making films since they left college.

q:
make
 Lisa and Sue are filmmakers. They ___ five films since they left college.
a: They have made five films since they left college.

q:
how long / learn / Arabic
 Your friend is learning Arabic. Ask them: ___?
a: How long have you been learning Arabic?

q:
wait / long
 Your friend is waiting for you. Ask her: ___?
a: Have you been waiting long?

q:
catch / any fish
 You see somebody fishing by the river. Ask them: ___?
a: Have you caught any fish?

q:
how many people / invite
 Your friends are having a party. Ask them: ___?
a: How many people have you invited?

q:
how long / teach
 Your friend is a teacher. Ask them: ___?
a: How long have you been teaching?

q:
how many books / write
 You meet a writer. Ask them: ___?
a: How many books have you written?

q:
how long / write / books
 You meet a writer. Ask them: ___?
a: How long have you been writing books?

q:
how long / save
 A friend is saving money for a trip. Ask them: ___?
a: How long have you been saving?

q:
how much money / save
 A friend is saving money for a trip. Ask them: ___?
a: How much money have you saved?

q:
somebody / break
 Look! ___ that window.
a: Look! Somebody has broken that window.

q:
you / work
 You look tired. ___ hard?
a: You look tired. Have you been working hard?

q:
you / ever / work
 '___ in a factory?' 'No, never.'
a: 'Have you ever worked in a factory?' 'No, never.'

q:
she / go
 Where's Lisa? Where ___?
a: Where's Lisa? Where has she gone?

q:
I / have
 This is a very old book. ___ it since I was a child.
a: This is a very old book. I have had it since I was a child.

q:
I / watch
 'Have you been busy?' 'No, ___ TV.'
a: 'Have you been busy?' 'No, I have been watching TV.'

q:
he / appear
 My brother is an actor. ___ in several films.
a: My brother is an actor. He has appeared in several films.

q:
I / not / wait
 'Sorry I'm late.' 'That's all right. ___ long.'
a: 'Sorry I'm late.' 'That's all right. I haven't been waiting long.'

q:
you / cry
 Are you OK? You look as if ___.
a: Are you OK? You look as if you have been crying.

q:
it / stop
 'Is it still raining?' 'No, ___.'
a: 'Is it still raining?' 'No, it has stopped.'

q:
they / play
 The children are tired now. ___ in the garden.
a: The children are tired now. They have been playing in the garden.

q:
I / lose
 I can't find my phone. ___ it.
a: I can't find my phone. I have lost my phone.

q:
I / read / not / finish
 ___ the book you lent me, but ___ it yet.
a: I have been reading the book you lent me, but I haven't finished it yet.

q:
you / see
 My phone is missing. ___ it?
a: My phone is missing. Have you seen it?

q:
I / read
 ___ the book you lent me, so you can have it back now.
a: I have read the book you lent me, so you can have it back now.
